contract.title: Will the CLARITY Act be signed into law in 2026?
contract.resolution_criteria:
This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025
(H.R.3633) is passed by both chambers of the U.S. Congress and signed into law by
December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to 'No'.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Congress.gov, the
White House, and the U.S. Government Publishing Office. A consensus of credible
reporting will also be used.

The CLARITY Act (also referred to as the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act) establishes
a regulatory framework distinguishing digital commodities from digital securities,
granting the CFTC primary jurisdiction over digital commodity spot markets and
clarifying the SEC's jurisdiction over digital asset securities.

conditions.label: H.R.3633 passed & signed by 12/31/26 ET
conditions.summary: H.R.3633 (Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025) passes both House and Senate and is signed into law by Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, confirmed by Congress.gov, White House, or GPO.

contract.narrative: SEC moves in March 2026 to apply securities laws to crypto dampen momentum for shifting jurisdiction, but an April staff statement on broker-dealer registration for crypto UIs revives pressure for statutory clarity. Concurrent CFTC actions, including suits against WI and MN and market-expanding steps, nudge Congress toward a CFTC-friendly CLARITY Act. Bill passage and presidential signature remain pending.

timeline.title: CFTC Sues Minnesota to Block State Law
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC suing Minnesota shows federal assertion over state crypto rules, modestly increasing odds Congress favors a federal CLARITY Act (bullish).

timeline.title: Rescission of Policy Regarding Denials in Settlements of Enforcement Actions
timeline.one_line_why: Securities and Exchange Commission — U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
timeline.effective_date: Date of Publication in the Federal Register

timeline.title: CME | Expansion of the Trading and ClearingHours for all Cryptocurrency Futures and Options on Futures Contracts. | [1]
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC expanding crypto futures trading hours underscores an active CFTC role in crypto markets, mildly increasing odds Congress favors a CFTC-friendly CLARITY Act.

timeline.title: GMNI - Liquidity Incentive Program - 05/07/2026
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC action involving Gemini is tangentially relevant to jurisdictional implementation debates but doesn't materially change chances H.R.3633 will become law.

timeline.title: CDNA - Amendments to rules on Insider definition, jurisdiction… - 05/06/2026
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC rule amendments reflect agency activity on crypto jurisdiction but are background regulatory action, not direct evidence of H.R.3633 being passed/signed.

timeline.title: 05.01.2026 - The changes amend Rules related to Bitnomial's non-FCM Clearing Members - BTNL
timeline.one_line_why: Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

timeline.title: CFTC Sues Wisconsin to Reaffirm its Exclusive Jurisdiction Over Prediction Markets
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC suing to assert exclusive jurisdiction over prediction markets bolsters its claim over digital markets, modestly increasing H.R.3633's chance (bullish).

timeline.title: GMNI Disclosure about AI information Features available to Members
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC disclosure about Gemini AI features shows active regulator involvement in crypto but is background noise that doesn't materially affect H.R.3633 passage odds.

timeline.title: 13th Annual Conference on Financial Market Regulation
timeline.regulator: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Economic and Risk Analysis
timeline.one_line_why: SEC conference on market regulation signals regulator engagement but provides no clear change to H.R.3633's chances of becoming law.

timeline.title: COIN | Crypto Market Maker Program - 04/16/2026
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC launching a Coinbase crypto market-maker program signals regulatory activity but doesn't materially change likelihood H.R.3633 is enacted.

timeline.title: COIN | Modifications to implement hard limits… - 04/16/2026
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC rule change on trading limits (COIN) is an agency action unrelated to Congress passing/signing H.R.3633; provides only minor background context.

timeline.title: Staff Statement Regarding Broker-Dealer Registration of Certain User Interfaces Utilized to Prepare Transactions in Crypto Asset Securities
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Trading and Markets
timeline.one_line_why: SEC staff asserting broker-dealer registration for crypto UIs likely increases pressure for Congress to enact clarifying law, raising odds H.R.3633 passes.

timeline.title: NPRM: Anti-Money Laundering and CFT Programs - 10.04.2026
timeline.one_line_why: FinCEN's AML/CFT NPRM shapes the digital-asset regulatory backdrop but does not directly affect whether H.R.3633 is passed and signed; low-impact background signal.

timeline.title: Administrative Summary - 2026-04-08 - Francis Decker, CPA
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Accounting and Auditing Enforcement
timeline.one_line_why: Securities and Exchange Commission — Accounting and Auditing Enforcement — U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

timeline.title: 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets
timeline.one_line_why: SEC guidance asserting securities coverage of crypto weakens the case for shifting spot jurisdiction to the CFTC, lowering odds that H.R.3633 passes.

timeline.title: Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets and Certain Transactions Involving Crypto Assets Corporation Finance
timeline.one_line_why: SEC guidance applying securities laws to crypto reduces congressional pressure to pass the CLARITY Act, lowering its passage odds.
timeline.effective_date: Date of publication in the Federal Register

timeline.title: COIN | Modifications to the Non-Crypto Market Maker Program
timeline.one_line_why: U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

timeline.title: CME - Implementation of the Chainlink, Stellar, & Cardano Futures Market Maker Program
timeline.one_line_why: Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

timeline.title: Secretary Bessent Announces Initiatives to Combat Rampant Fraud in Minnesota
timeline.one_line_why: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) — Financial Crimes Enforcement Network: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

timeline.title: Acting Chairman Pham Announces Launch of Digital Assets Pilot Program for Tokenized Collateral in Derivatives Markets
timeline.regulator: Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Market Participants Division, Division of Market Oversight, Division of Clearing and Risk
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC pilot shows regulator activity on digital assets but is not direct evidence for or against H.R.3633 becoming law.

primary_drivers: Enforcement & litigation: CFTC suit and rescission of settlement-denial policy
heat_panel.explainer: Critical heat driven by concentrated enforcement and rulemaking in crypto/payments — major lawsuits and settlement-policy changes, AML/stablecoin NPRMs, and exchange/market-maker program shifts, though down vs last week.
